title: Uber SSE Backend (L5 A)
content:
Hey Guys, 
Need Help in Analysing the offer from Uber for SSE (L5-A) role. 

Base: 76.7L + 4.6(PF) = 81.3L
Stocks: 102K USD (front loaded 35% first year) ~= 33.9L
Performance Bonus: 15.3L (recruiter said its minimum and usually people get more than this always)

Consdering all these numbers 
Total Comp: ~= 1.3 Cr (added perf bonus also in this)

Current Comp
Designation: SSE (will be promoated to PE in next 2 months)
Base: 58.2L (appraisal are yet to be done in june/july)
Bonus: 16L (last year, next year might be less)
ESops: 168K USD (back loaded) ~= 25L (next year) (buybacks do happen but usually once within 2-3 years with upto 20-30% of buyback)

YOE: 8

title: FAANG companies - Open to Top Tech Opportunities
content:
I’m currently working in Riga as a Software Engineer and actively looking to take the next step in my career.

I’m passionate about building scalable backend systems, working with modern web technologies, and solving real-world problems through clean and efficient code. Over the past period, I’ve been focusing on strengthening my skills in Java Spring Boot, React, and full-stack system design.

Now I’m aiming to join a top-tier company where I can contribute to impactful products, collaborate with talented engineers, and continue growing in a challenging environment.

If you know of any opportunities or can point me in the right direction, I would really appreciate your support or connection.

Open to:

Backend / Full-stack roles
Remote or EU-based opportunities
Product-focused engineering teams

Thanks in advance!

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Data Scientist Interview Experience
content:
### 📌 Interview Experience – Kotak Mahindra Bank - Data Science / SDE1 (1 YOE)

I recently went through the interview process for a Data Science – SDE1 role. Sharing my experience in case it helps others.

---

### 🧠 **Round 1 (L1 – Bar Raiser round)**

Focused heavily on core concepts:

* Difference between **method vs function**
* Difference between **range vs xrange**
* **Stratified sampling**
* Train vs validation vs test sets
* **Regularization**
* **Gradient descent**
* Role of optimizers and types
* **Bias-variance tradeoff**
* Tensor vs NumPy array in deep learning
* Debugging shape mismatch issues
* Monitoring training (early problem detection)
* Training mode vs evaluation mode

**LLM & GenAI topics:**

* Tokenization and its impact
* Temperature in LLMs
* Common LLM failures
* RAG vs fine-tuning
* Hallucination mitigation
* Context window
* Embeddings & grounding
* Model inversion & data poisoning
* Handling stale/new docs in vector DB
* Prompt injection & adversarial inputs
* Sensitive data leakage (outputs/logs)
* Securing RAG pipelines

---

### 💻 **Round 2 (L2 – Practical / Hands-on)**

Scheduled ~1 month after L1.

* Given multiple datasets in Google Colab
* Tasks involved:

  * Data manipulation (groupby, aggregations, etc.)
  * Business interpretation questions


Also included interpretation-based reasoning like:

* Impact of discounting on profitability

---

### 📄 **Round 3 (L3 – Resume Deep Dive)**

~1 week after L2

* Entirely based on past projects and work experience


---

### 🧾 **HR Round**

~10 days later

* Notice period
* Compensation discussion
* General HR questions

---

### ✅ **Verdict: Selected (Waiting for Offer Letter)**

---

### ❓ Questions for the community:

1. How long does it usually take to receive the offer letter after the HR round?
2. How is the company in terms of:

   * Work-life balance
   * Compensation
   * Benefits

title: Paytm SSE Offer Letter Pending After Verbal Confirmation - Need Advice
content:
Hi everyone,

  I recently cleared all rounds for Senior Software Engineer role at Paytm for the Recharge and Utilities team and received a congratulations mail. The salary negotiation was also completed, and HR verbally agreed on around 24 LPA.

  However, it has been around 17 days and the official offer letter has still not been released. HR is saying the offer is currently in a pending approval state, but they are not aware of the exact reason.
  They also mentioned that it might not get approved, possibly due to internal numbers/budget, and because of that they cannot provide a Letter of Intent or any written confirmation.

  Has anyone else faced this with Paytm or similar companies? Is this normal in their process? I wanted to understand how common this situation is in the current market.


  Any advice from people who have gone through Paytm’s hiring process or similar situations would be really helpful.

title: Arcesium SDET-1 (DSA Round) – What to Expect?
content:
Hi everyone,

I have an interview with Arcesium on May 8, 2026, for the role of SDET-1 (Test Engineering). The interview will be with a Lead Engineer, and HR mentioned that it will be a DSA round.

Here’s my process so far:
* Applied via Instahyre (April 23)
* Gave OA on April 24:
  * 13 MCQs (basic LLD + DSA) — attempted 12/13
  * 2 DSA questions (basic math + interval problem) — solved using brute force
* Got shortlisted on May 5

Can anyone share what kind of questions I can expect or their interview experience for this role?

---

Any help or tips would really mean a lot. Thanks!
